230234.explanation: JA21 finds it unfair that status holders get priority for social housing. Residents of Leeuwarden should not be put last. This priority must be abolished.

230235.explanation: JA21 believes that those allowed to stay in the Netherlands are obliged to learn Dutch and participate in society. Language lessons are not a free provision but a personal responsibility. Those who want to integrate should also invest in it themselves.

230236.explanation: JA21 believes that labor migration must not lead to housing shortages and nuisance in residential areas. Employers who recruit foreign workers must therefore provide proper housing themselves. The costs and responsibility belong to the company, not the community.

230237.explanation: JA21 supports greenery in the city, but safety and visibility must always come first. Along busy roads, trees must not endanger visibility and traffic safety. Green policies must be realistic and responsible.

230238.explanation: JA21 believes residents should be able to decide how they heat their homes. A general ban on fireplaces and wood stoves goes too far. Personal responsibility and common sense come first.

230239.explanation: JA21 opposes replacing private fireworks with a municipal show. Fireworks are a tradition and belong to New Year’s Eve. Banning or taking over by the municipality is not the solution.

230241.explanation: JA21 values the Frisian language and identity. Attention to Frisian in primary schools is important, as long as schools allow room for customization and it does not become a mandatory straitjacket.

230243.explanation: JA21 is cautious about expanding the number of coffee shops. More coffee shops often lead to extra nuisance and crime. Safety and livability must be paramount.

230244.explanation: JA21 sees camera surveillance as an effective means against nuisance and crime. In places where problems are structural, camera surveillance should be expanded. Safety comes first.

230246.explanation: JA21 is against building quotas. A mandatory minimum of 40% social housing makes building more expensive, slower, and can even cause projects to be unfeasible for developers. The focus should be on building faster and achieving a balanced housing mix.

230247.explanation: The housing shortage requires a quick solution. Building upwards can also be necessary, especially within the city. However, high-rise buildings must fit the environment and be accompanied by good accessibility and sufficient parking.

230248.explanation: JA21 is cautious about extra rules in the housing market. The housing shortage is solved by building faster and more, not by imposing new obligations on buyers. Fewer rules and more supply help starters the most.

230249.explanation: JA21 supports transforming empty offices into (student) housing. This primarily belongs to the market. JA21 is cautious about using tax money for real estate projects. Municipal funds should be spent on core tasks such as safety and basic services.

230251.explanation: JA21 finds it important that everyone who can contributes to society. It is unacceptable that people receive benefits without doing anything, while many Leeuwarden residents work hard and struggle to make ends meet.

230252.explanation: JA21 is critical of subsidized jobs that keep people dependent on the government. The focus should be on guidance to regular work. Jobs should offer perspective, not be temporarily maintained by subsidies.

230253.explanation: JA21 wants municipal taxes to be as low as possible for everyone. For residents who really cannot pay, remission should remain possible. This must be done carefully and remain fair to Leeuwarden citizens who carry their own burden.

230254.explanation: For many residents, the car is indispensable. A shortage of parking spaces causes irritation and nuisance in residential areas. The municipality must ensure sufficient parking so neighborhoods remain livable.

230255.explanation: Parking should not be a revenue model for the municipality. Lower rates make the city more accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs and contribute to a lively city center.

230256.explanation: JA21 believes shops and entrepreneurs should be well supplied. General bans on commercial and freight vehicles make this harder and especially affect SMEs. Policy should be realistic and keep the city accessible.

230257.explanation: Residents who drive electric must be able to charge their car. Additional charging stations are possible, as long as this is demand-driven and does not lead to extra costs or parking pressure.

230258.explanation: In villages, the car is often necessary for work, care, and amenities. Free parking is part of the livability and accessibility of villages and must be maintained.

230259.explanation: JA21 is against making the city center more car-free. This harms accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. The car remains necessary and should not be chased away by ideological policies without support.

230260.explanation: The need for amenities differs per neighborhood and village. JA21 is against one-size-fits-all solutions and believes the municipality must provide customization without unnecessary costs.

230261.explanation: A clear separation between pedestrians, cyclists, and cars increases safety. JA21 is against shared spaces because these cause unnecessary insecurity. Around the station, a clear and logical layout is necessary.

230262.explanation: Where roads permit, traffic flow must be improved. A higher speed can reduce congestion, provided this is done safely and responsibly.

230263.explanation: JA21 believes the owner should decide what they do with their property. The municipality must be restrained with fines and coercion. The housing shortage is solved by building faster, not by limiting property rights. However, the municipality should actively engage in discussions in case of deterioration due to vacancy.

230265.explanation: JA21 values sports clubs highly for health, community spirit, and volunteer work. Sports thrive in Leeuwarden and all clubs contribute to this. Frisian sports are part of our identity, but municipal sports policy must be unifying and treat all sports equally.

230268.explanation: JA21 wants to allocate cultural funds to initiatives with clear local and social utility and support. Large, costly events often yield little concrete for residents and therefore have less priority.

230270.explanation: JA21 finds it important that children and young people become familiar with culture and history. Free access to museums lowers barriers and contributes to education and development.

230271.explanation: JA21 believes entrepreneurs who take responsibility for safety deserve support. Joint investments in safety strengthen business parks and prevent crime.

230272.explanation: JA21 believes sustainability efforts should be voluntary and feasible. The municipality should not provide subsidies without clear added value. Farmers need certainty, not extra rules.

230274.explanation: JA21 is against extra prohibitions in public spaces. Responsibility primarily lies with the provider and the user. Education is more effective than patronizing.

230275.explanation: JA21 believes aid should be targeted at people who really need it. Raising thresholds makes schemes more expensive and less focused. The focus must be on work and self-reliance.

230277.explanation: JA21 finds safety important outside the built-up area as well. Good lighting prevents unsafe situations and increases residents' sense of security.

230278.explanation: JA21 believes parents are primarily responsible for caring for their children. Structural provisions through schools are not part of this. Help should focus on families who really need it.

230279.explanation: JA21 wants municipal charges to be reduced. Waste processing must be efficient and affordable. Residents should not keep bearing rising costs.

230281.explanation: JA21 is cautious about creating new functions and counters. Problems must be solved within existing structures. Extra layers do not improve government.

230284.explanation: JA21 believes sport and culture must be accessible to everyone. Targeted support helps people participate and strengthens health and social cohesion.

230285.explanation: JA21 is critical of housing projects that try to combine social goals with housing construction. The housing shortage is not solved with experiments but by building enough affordable homes. The focus should be on housing for young people and families from Leeuwarden itself.

275822.explanation: JA21 finds it important that elderly people can live independently as long as possible. Supporting appropriate housing forms with care improves quality of life and prevents heavier and more expensive care in the long term.

275823.explanation: JA21 believes residents should be able to make their own choices about sustainability. Deploying energy coaches is patronizing and causes unnecessary extra costs. Municipal funds should be used to reduce burdens, not to steer residents in personal choices.

275824.explanation: JA21 believes the municipality should set other priorities first. The renovation of the Prins Hendrik Bridge is very costly and offers limited benefits. Community funds can be better spent on safety, housing, and maintenance of existing infrastructure.

275825.explanation: JA21 believes the discussion should not be about the location of an asylum seekers center but about reducing asylum reception in Leeuwarden. The municipality should focus on phasing out and closing existing centers, not on finding new locations.

275826.explanation: JA21 believes reception is intended for people who need protection and must always be temporary. The goal is return to the country of origin as soon as this is safe. Municipal reception for people who are not allowed to stay here is unacceptable.

275827.explanation: JA21 is against the establishment of a new asylum seekers center in Leeuwarden. The municipality should instead focus on phasing out and closing existing centers. Leeuwarden already contributes enough and cannot keep organizing extra reception.

230227.explanation: A dignified life includes having control over your own environment. Therefore, it should ultimately be the population as a whole— not council members— who decide what happens and what does not. Currently, the threshold for residents to speak out is too high. The SP wants to make this easier.

230229.explanation: The SP stands for an affordable Leeuwarden, even if you own a house. We do not solve money shortages caused by bad decisions in The Hague by making the residents pay the price. We will tackle profiteering landlords; those who simply have a home should come out ahead.

230230.explanation: Everyday life is becoming increasingly expensive. This is not a natural phenomenon, but the result of political choices. The municipality does not need to add to this burden for those who want to visit our municipality.

230231.explanation: Everyday life is becoming increasingly expensive. This is not a natural phenomenon, but the result of political choices. The municipality should not make things worse for those who happen to have a dog. Pollution and nuisance should be solved by the community, not by turning dog owners into cash cows.

230232.explanation: The inner city is more than just a nightlife spot. It is also a neighborhood where thousands of people live and form a community. The municipality must first listen to the wishes of the inner city residents before giving hospitality venues a free pass.

230233.explanation: Our climate is being destroyed by capitalism. The only way to fight this is through collective changes, not individual ones that increase class differences. The SP advocates for a joint energy company owned by all residents and a municipality that supports solidarity.

230234.explanation: Everyone deserves a good home. We, as the working class, will not let ourselves be divided while profiteering landlords and land speculators enrich themselves. The municipality must stand by everyone who needs a home and ensure humane treatment, including for status holders (refugees with residency status).

230235.explanation: Only by understanding each other can we form a community. Those who flee here from war and violence are treated with dignity and are expected to learn the language. The municipality must also make that possible.

230236.explanation: Migrant workers are widely exploited by unscrupulous employers and labor brokers. Too often, workers depend on their boss for both income and housing. We do not want to increase this unequal power balance but reduce it. That starts with having enough homes for everyone.

230237.explanation: Greening our city and villages makes our municipality livable and cooler in the summer. By planting more trees where it is safe to do so, we address biodiversity loss, climate change, and nitrogen problems.

230238.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves should remain possible, provided they meet requirements to limit nuisance and health damage. People should never be left to fend for their own warmth. For that, we organize collective cheap energy through a non-profit municipal energy company.

230239.explanation: Many people enjoy fireworks every year. As consumer fireworks are gradually reduced, we can celebrate New Year’s with an accessible, safe, and responsible fireworks show, resulting in less nuisance.

230241.explanation: The Frisian language is an important part of identity for many people and of the region. Knowledge of Frisian language and culture enriches and promotes broad development.

230243.explanation: It is time for a different drug policy. You do not combat nuisance, addiction, and crime by banning coffee shops but by investing in care services and no longer forcing coffee shop owners to operate under a legal gray area.

230244.explanation: Cameras are a major intrusion on people’s privacy and often ineffective. The SP prefers having eyes and ears in the streets and neighborhoods.

230246.explanation: Affordable housing in Leeuwarden is increasingly becoming a privilege and unattainable for people with average or low incomes, while land speculators and profiteering landlords get richer. Every new neighborhood must therefore, according to the SP, consist of at least fifty percent affordable rental homes.

230247.explanation: The SP advocates for more mixed neighborhoods: that means more affordable rental homes in areas where mostly more expensive houses currently stand. Higher residential flats with well-maintained homes can be a solution.

230248.explanation: A house is for living in, not for getting rich from. Profiteering landlords who enrich themselves at the expense of victims of the housing crisis have no place in Leeuwarden, according to the SP. This begins by requiring that whoever buys a house must also live in it.

230249.explanation: There are thousands of square meters of office space empty across the country, while there is a severe shortage of homes. This causes young people to live at home longer and start families later. By converting long-term vacant offices, we can provide more housing.

230251.explanation: Those who work deserve a fair wage. No one chooses to live on social assistance. It is a last safety net to lead a dignified life. We choose real jobs, with real wages and sufficient support to find work.

230252.explanation: Unemployment is not an individual failure but a shared responsibility. Meaningful work can give people satisfaction. For those who find this harder to achieve themselves, we have an obligation to help them. This is how we organize solidarity.

230253.explanation: Letting debts pile up does not solve poverty. Writing off debts does break the spiral. With debt relief, guidance, and barring for-profit debt management companies, we can lift more children out of poverty.

230254.explanation: For many people, a car is indispensable for their work, education, or social life. As long as there is still too little affordable and reliable public transport, parking spaces remain a necessary part of neighborhoods.

230255.explanation: Everyday life is becoming more expensive in all areas. By lowering parking fees as a municipality, we help keep life livable. With an unreliable and expensive public transport system, many people remain dependent on cars.

230256.explanation: The SP stands for a livable and healthy municipality. Driving up costs for shopkeepers and small entrepreneurs without an alternative does not bring us closer to a cleaner world.

230257.explanation: Charging stations should be a communal facility. By placing more charging stations where necessary as a municipality, we offer collective solutions to the climate problem instead of leaving people at the mercy of commercial charging stations.

230258.explanation: For many people, a car is indispensable for their work, education, or social life. As long as there is still too little affordable and reliable public transport, especially in the villages, free parking spaces contribute to accessibility.

230259.explanation: For many people, a car is indispensable for their work, education, or social life. This also applies to entrepreneurs in the inner city. The inner city must remain accessible to them and visitors, including by car.

230260.explanation: Community spirit is a great asset. Places to come together for free with your village or neighborhood neighbors, besides your work, school, or home, are essential for social cohesion and livability.

230261.explanation: The SP wants the municipality to conduct thorough research into the safety and satisfaction regarding the shared space areas. The outcomes must be guiding.

230262.explanation: A higher maximum speed does not save time but causes more accidents and pollution.

230263.explanation: A house is for living in, not for making a profit. Our homes are too important to be left in the hands of investors and profiteering landlords. In case of repeated refusal, the SP wants the municipality to have the power to expropriate.

230265.explanation: The Frisian language and culture are an important part of identity for many people. Promoting that culture enriches our society.

230268.explanation: Culture enriches and uplifts. The SP firmly supports cultural initiatives. However, Arcadia seems to miss the point, with high costs and few visitors. That money can better be invested in accessible initiatives in neighborhoods and villages.

230270.explanation: Culture enriches and uplifts. Ticket prices can be a major barrier for young people visiting museums.

230271.explanation: Safety is a collective goal, not an individual problem. The municipality must be an ally of residents who want to jointly improve their living, working, or living environment.

230272.explanation: Many farmers are trapped in an economic system that pushes for ever larger scale and more debts. Where possible, the municipality should break this stranglehold and help farmers to become more sustainable, so they can produce a fair product for a fair price.

230274.explanation: The SP supports a strict gambling policy. On our initiative, a ban on online gambling advertisements has been introduced. We want to extend this ban to a total prohibition on gambling ads.

230275.explanation: Poverty is not an individual failure but a problem for all of us. That is why we organize solidarity and give an extra push where needed. We owe this to the over 1,000 children who still grow up in poverty in Leeuwarden.

230277.explanation: Social safety also means a safe environment. By installing more street lamps in poorly lit areas, we all feel safer, alongside investments in enforcement, social work, and prevention.

230278.explanation: Every child deserves a full stomach. In Leeuwarden, we do not let any child sit hungry in class. A free sandwich at school is the least we can do, alongside all SP plans to eradicate poverty.

230279.explanation: Everyday life is becoming increasingly expensive. This is not a natural phenomenon but the result of political choices. Paying per bag of waste (DIFTAR) leads to more inequality and more pollution. We choose a solidarity levy, where those with the greatest capacity bear the heaviest burdens.

230281.explanation: A reliable government starts with a municipality that truly listens to its residents, including young people and children. By taking their voices seriously, we organize participation.

230284.explanation: Culture and sports enrich our lives and promote social cohesion. This should not be a privilege for people with higher incomes but a provision for everyone. That is why we organize it as a society, through the municipality.

230285.explanation: For good integration of newcomers, it is necessary that they learn the language and live with us, instead of living alongside us. Initiatives that promote this will receive our support.

275822.explanation: Being able to grow old in a dignified way is becoming a luxury reserved only for those who can afford it. We believe it is a right for everyone. That is why we want suitable homes and small-scale care neighborhood centers for those who can no longer live independently.

275823.explanation: Combating climate change is not an individual problem but something we must do as a society. The municipality should stand alongside the residents and help where it can, with energy coaches and with its own housing association offering well-insulated houses and low rents.

275824.explanation: Leeuwarden is a beautiful and attractive city. We like to invest in that. But every proposal for a new bridge proves to be so expensive that it comes at the expense of desperately needed facilities for people in Leeuwarden. We choose no children in poverty over a new bridge.

275825.explanation: The city government has botched the plans so much that the court has now ruled that next to the airbase is not a suitable location for an asylum seekers’ center (azc). The SP stands for humane accommodation for those fleeing war and violence, without noise nuisance and appropriate for the neighborhood. That should be somewhere else.

275826.explanation: Those who do not flee violence or persecution and have been told by the court that they may not stay must return. But politics has cut so hard on the people who must arrange that return that it often cannot be done. We do not let anyone sleep on the street, so for now we arrange dignified shelter.

275827.explanation: The SP is clear: we help and humanely house people fleeing war and violence, but those who have no right to be here must return as soon as possible. Refugees should be accommodated more often by richer municipalities. Until that is arranged, we help do our fair share in Leeuwarden.

230227.explanation: BBB approves that citizens can request a referendum. Properly organizing a referendum takes a lot of time and is expensive. Therefore, it is good that applicants first demonstrate with sufficient signatures that there is genuine demand for a referendum.

230229.explanation: Paying taxes is a necessary evil. The government, and thus also the municipality of Leeuwarden, must handle taxpayers' money as carefully as possible so that citizens and businesses do not pay more than necessary.

230230.explanation: Leeuwarden has a lot to offer tourists, from water sports to culture. The municipality contributes community funds to many facilities. It is fair that tourists also contribute to this via the tourist tax.

230231.explanation: Dog tax has already been abolished by most municipalities. The revenues do not go to facilities or costs for dogs but to the general funds. BBB sees no reason to keep this tax in place any longer for some dog owners in the municipality.

230232.explanation: People also live in the city center, and noise from café visitors is part of city living. But for livability, it is good that there is a closing time.

230233.explanation: It is great if citizens want to invest together in sustainability. The government should certainly support and facilitate that. But participants must know what they are getting into. Every investment carries financial risks, and those risks should not fall on the taxpayer.

230234.explanation: BBB finds it incomprehensible that residents who have been on a waiting list for social rental housing for years are passed over simply because someone else has refugee status.

230235.explanation: Integration and civic integration are the personal responsibilities of the people who want to live here. This also applies to learning the language.

230236.explanation: BBB prefers that seasonal workers can live on or near their employer’s premises. This prevents seasonal work from putting extra pressure on available housing in villages and neighborhoods.

230237.explanation: Trees can be beautiful and provide shade and cooling in the summer. But in the open landscape that our municipality belongs to, trees do not fit everywhere. This also makes the landscape less suitable for meadow birds and other species we do want to preserve.

230238.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden is against a ban on wood stoves. It is everyone’s own responsibility to burn responsibly and prevent nuisance.

230239.explanation: Fireworks are part of the Dutch New Year's tradition, just like doughnuts and carbide shooting. A municipal fireworks show can be a nice alternative now that fireworks are banned nationally.

230241.explanation: Our language is part of our culture and who we are. BBB strongly supports a prominent position for Frisian and regional languages, including in primary schools.

230243.explanation: Drug use has become far too normal. It is embarrassing that Leeuwarden is champion in cocaine use. If we want to reduce drug use, we must stop normalizing it. More sales points for soft drugs do not fit with that.

230244.explanation: Camera surveillance can help increase safety and track down perpetrators of crimes and offenses.

230246.explanation: A higher share of social rental housing comes at the expense of affordable owner-occupied or mid-rent homes. Moreover, it makes housing plans even less profitable, causing them to be canceled or the municipality to have to invest much extra money.

230247.explanation: Space is scarce and expensive; by building higher, we can realize extra homes without needing more space. Furthermore, due to aging and smaller households, there is more demand for apartments.

230248.explanation: Large investments in the housing market are needed to solve the housing shortage. Private parties investing in building or making homes sustainable to then rent them out are essential. Using a home as a vacation or second home is not acceptable according to BBB.

230249.explanation: There is a great shortage of student housing, while many thousands of square meters of office space stand empty. Converting office space into student housing is an excellent solution in which the municipality can also invest.

230251.explanation: Those who receive an income from society should give something back. This is not only just but also ensures that people remain actively involved in their environment.

230252.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden does not believe in subsidized jobs. Experience shows that such people do not move faster to regular jobs.

230253.explanation: Paying taxes is part of it and applies to everyone. But a farming saying is that you cannot pluck a bald chicken. Trying to collect taxes from someone who truly has no money is pointless and exacerbates problems around poverty and debt.

230254.explanation: In a rural municipality like Leeuwarden, many people need a car for work or other reasons. If roads in residential neighborhoods are blocked by parked cars, this creates unsafe situations. Sufficient parking spaces are simply necessary.

230255.explanation: Paid parking should not become a municipal cash cow. But without paid parking, there would be a lot of parking nuisance in the city. The current rates in Leeuwarden are not high compared to other municipalities.

230256.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden is against emission zones. It drives entrepreneurs out of the city and residents into high costs.

230257.explanation: Residents who cannot park on their own property and have no public charging station nearby can now request one from the municipality. BBB Leeuwarden wants this to be easy but is not in favor of installing charging stations indiscriminately. That is wasteful of money and grid space.

230258.explanation: For livability and preserving facilities in villages such as shops, it is important that residents can continue to park easily and for free.

230259.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden sees no reason to make more of the city center car-free. Shopkeepers, entrepreneurs, and residents must also remain accessible by car.

230260.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden believes that the municipality together with residents of villages and neighborhoods should ensure that village halls and community centers are preserved.

230261.explanation: BBB believes it must be clear to all road users who has right of way where. The pedestrian crossing and bike crossing at the station are a good step, but there are also other places with shared spaces where the situation is too unclear and confusing.

230262.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden wants a maximum speed that suits the situation. On the Overijsselselaan but also the Drachtsterweg, BBB believes the speed limit could be raised from 50 to 70 km/h.

230263.explanation: We have a housing shortage, and therefore leaving homes empty for a long time is not acceptable. Moreover, vacant homes do not contribute to a livable neighborhood. If owners choose to leave their property empty for extended periods, in extreme cases a fine should be imposed.

230265.explanation: For sports that belong to Fryslân, such as kaatsen, fierljeppen, sailing (including skûtsjesilen), and skating, among others in the Elfstedenhal, BBB wants specific attention in the municipal sports policy and, where necessary, additional investments.

230268.explanation: The latest edition of Arcadia cost the municipality too much extra money and delivered too little. BBB believes it's time to stop it and spend the money differently.

230270.explanation: The museum is a wonderful place to learn about our history, culture, and environment. Many museums already offer free access for children or on certain days. BBB supports ensuring that our children can visit any museum for free.

230271.explanation: The municipality is responsible for safety in public spaces, and companies are responsible for safety on their own premises. If they want to invest together in this, that's great, but it does not mean the municipality has to pay for it.

230272.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden opposes additional municipal sustainability rules or targets for farmers or other businesses on top of national or European policies. However, if the municipality requires sustainability investments from businesses, the municipality should also pay for it.

230274.explanation: BBB does not like patronizing behavior and extra rules. Preventing the cigar shop from promoting a lottery ticket or the village pub from advertising should not be a municipal priority.

230275.explanation: BBB Leeuwarden wants every resident to get by decently. A good poverty policy is part of that. That assistance must reach the people who need it most and aim to help them escape poverty and regain sufficient independent income.

230277.explanation: Good street lighting ensures traffic safety and that everyone can safely walk outside after dark. Therefore, BBB wants enough street lamps inside and outside built-up areas. This can also be done with smart lighting that turns on when someone is nearby.

230278.explanation: Going to school without a good breakfast negatively affects children's learning and opportunities. BBB Leeuwarden supports a school breakfast. Together with schools and national initiatives, we want to explore how to organize this.

230279.explanation: Waste collection charges are meant to cover the costs of collecting and processing our waste. This simply costs money. BBB Leeuwarden wants this to be done as cheaply as possible so the costs and thus the waste collection charges remain affordable for everyone.

230281.explanation: The municipality of Leeuwarden must handle complaints properly itself. If that goes wrong, residents can go to the national ombudsman, who has all the expertise needed. Having a separate municipal ombudsman, with or without youth focus, is not an improvement.

230284.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in sports and society, but good arrangements already exist that, according to BBB Leeuwarden, do not need to be expanded. It is especially important that everyone who needs them can find these arrangements.

230285.explanation: The municipality must provide sufficient housing for all home seekers, including youth and status holders. But deciding who lives with whom should not be part of municipal policy.

275822.explanation: BBB wants new-style nursing homes where people can live independently but with the care, support, and companionship they need.

275823.explanation: Companies and, for example, energy suppliers can provide advice on energy saving just fine; the municipality does not have to do this. We prefer to spend money on measures that truly reduce citizens' energy bills rather than on coaches.

275824.explanation: According to BBB, the added value of a movable bridge does not outweigh the costs.

275825.explanation: Provided sufficient consideration is given to Defense and local residents, BBB is not fundamentally against this location. Conditions that always apply for BBB are: good consultation with the surroundings, no large numbers, and strict agreements to prevent nuisance.

275826.explanation: Those who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands should leave the country; accommodation is not appropriate.

275827.explanation: Leeuwarden has emergency shelter for asylum seekers, which will likely be replaced by an asylum seekers' center near the airbase. According to BBB, that is enough.

230227.explanation: SL!M believes it is important that residents can co-decide on policies that affect them. The threshold of 300 signatures for a referendum is high. Lowering it gives more people a voice and encourages involvement.

230229.explanation: SL!M understands that no one likes paying taxes, but the property tax (OZB) is needed to pay for facilities everyone uses, such as community centers, sports, and culture. Since OZB is already low in Leeuwarden, we consider any further reduction unwise.

230230.explanation: SL!M is happy with visitors in our city and villages. The tourist tax in Leeuwarden is already low and helps pay for facilities that tourists also use. This keeps Leeuwarden hospitable and social.

230231.explanation: SL!M opposes abolishing the dog tax. Leeuwarden has many dog walking areas and facilities that help keep neighborhoods clean. These are partly paid for by dog owners. This keeps neighborhoods livable and prevents everyone from sharing the costs.

230232.explanation: SL!M believes hospitality entrepreneurs should set their own closing times. This allows entrepreneurship and ensures a lively city center. Different closing times spread visitors and can reduce nuisance, while the municipality continues to monitor safety.

230233.explanation: SL!M believes residents who jointly generate sustainable energy deserve financial support. This helps good grassroots ideas take off faster, keeps revenues local, and strengthens social cohesion, while residents retain control over their sustainable future.

230234.explanation: SL!M believes social rental homes should go to those most urgently in need, regardless of origin. Tailored solutions prevent vulnerable groups from being left behind, help refugees and other residents quickly, and promote solidarity without polarization.

230235.explanation: SL!M believes newcomers should be able to participate quickly. Free language lessons help them become independent, find work, and build contacts. Investing in language education means investing in participation, connection, and equal opportunities for all.

230236.explanation: SL!M believes living and working should remain separate. Seasonal workers must live safely and independently of their workplace. Employer-provided housing increases the risk of abuses. Moreover, the farmer is often not the employer but an employment agency.

230237.explanation: SL!M believes more trees along Leeuwarden’s roads provide healthy air, calm, and nature experience. Well-placed trees can naturally slow traffic and increase biodiversity. This makes the city greener, healthier, and safer for everyone.

230238.explanation: SL!M believes Leeuwarden residents should be allowed to choose fireplaces and wood stoves, as long as they consider their neighbors. A ban is unnecessary; we believe in freedom, mutual respect, and living together without additional restrictive rules.

230239.explanation: SL!M believes a municipal fireworks show on New Year's Eve is a beautiful way to celebrate the new year together. Freely accessible to everyone, safely organized, and without extra nuisance, it strengthens community spirit and coziness in the city.

230241.explanation: SL!M is proud of the Frisian language and wants to preserve it. Nevertheless, the party believes primary schools should decide how they offer Frisian. The municipality can support with information and resources, but schools should remain free to choose.

230243.explanation: SL!M believes there are enough coffeeshops in Leeuwarden. New locations are unnecessary and can cause nuisance. The current municipal ban helps keep oversight and ensures safety and livability for residents and visitors.

230244.explanation: SL!M believes Leeuwarden residents should feel safe. More camera surveillance can detect crime and increase safety, but privacy remains important. Clarity about the use and access to footage is essential; surveillance is for safety, not for amusement.

230246.explanation: SL!M believes many more social rental homes must be built in Leeuwarden. With waiting times of 6–8 years for affordable housing, increasing the share to 40% is necessary to give starters, small households, and low-income people a chance and to relieve market pressure.

230247.explanation: SL!M believes high-rise flats in Leeuwarden can help solve the housing crisis. They offer affordable housing for students, starters, and families without further expanding the city. High-rise building must consider quality of life and the cityscape.

230248.explanation: SL!M believes homes in Leeuwarden are for people, not investors. Anyone who buys a house should live in it themselves. This keeps the market fair, prevents unnecessary price increases, and gives starters and families a fair chance at affordable housing.

230249.explanation: Vacancy is good for no one. Students meanwhile find it difficult to get housing. SL!M thinks it is a good idea to convert vacant offices into student housing. This helps combat the housing crisis and contributes to a livable city.

230251.explanation: SL!M believes that people receiving social assistance benefits should be supported to contribute to society, for example through volunteer work. However, making this mandatory is not appropriate; involvement grows from motivation, not coercion. Investing in guidance and tailored support empowers people.

230252.explanation: SL!M believes that the long-term unemployed should be given more opportunities. Subsidized jobs help them gain work experience, develop skills, and participate again in society. This way, the municipality supports residents while also strengthening the local economy.

230253.explanation: SL!M believes that the waiver of municipal taxes for residents with low incomes should remain. It reduces financial stress, prevents debt, and ensures that everyone can participate in society. This is how we combat poverty, which is far too high in Leeuwarden.

230254.explanation: SL!M is positive about more parking spaces in residential neighborhoods, but not at the expense of green areas and playgrounds. With every new parking spot, a balance must be struck between living environment and accessibility, so that residents and the neighborhood truly benefit.

230255.explanation: SL!M finds affordable parking important, but any reduction must be done carefully. Leeuwarden already has low rates, and more cars reduce available space. We want to promote accessibility and safe cycling routes, so residents can consciously choose how they travel.

230256.explanation: SL!M wants cleaner air and a livable center, but an immediate ban on polluting commercial and freight vehicles could severely impact businesses. A phased transition with clear rules and grace periods gives companies time to prepare.

230257.explanation: SL!M believes the municipality should focus on sustainable mobility. More charging stations for electric vehicles make switching from fossil fuels easier and contribute to cleaner air, a better environment, and a future-proof city.

230258.explanation: SL!M believes that parking in the villages of Leeuwarden should remain free. This supports lively village centers, keeps streets accessible, and helps local entrepreneurs. This way, the villages remain attractive, accessible, and pleasant for residents and visitors.

230259.explanation: SL!M believes that more car-free streets make the inner city livable, safe, and attractive. Children can play, pedestrians and cyclists gain space, and the air becomes cleaner. At the same time, residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs must retain good access through smart solutions.

230260.explanation: SL!M believes that every neighborhood and village in Leeuwarden deserves a social and cultural community center. Centers like Netwerkcentrum De Klomp offer a place to meet, do activities together, and receive support. They strengthen social bonds and community life.

230261.explanation: SL!M believes that clear zones for pedestrians, cyclists, and cars are needed around Leeuwarden station. Shared space has led to confusing situations and accidents. Separate zones make traffic safer and create a more pleasant station environment.

230262.explanation: SL!M opposes increasing the speed limit on Overijsselselaan to 70 km/h. There are already many accidents and violations. The existing green wave for 50 km/h and enforcement ensure safe and smooth traffic flow. Safety comes first.

230263.explanation: SL!M believes that homes in Leeuwarden should not remain unnecessarily empty. A fine for prolonged vacancy encourages owners to rent or sell houses. This makes more homes available for families, youth, and vulnerable groups.

230265.explanation: SL!M is proud of Frisian identity and heritage but believes the municipality should not favor specific sports. All sports and cultural associations deserve equal opportunities for support, regardless of origin or popularity.

230268.explanation: SL!M values culture and supports Arcadia. The event brings residents and visitors together, enriches cultural life, and strengthens tourism, the economy, and social cohesion. Therefore, we oppose reducing financial support for Arcadia.

230270.explanation: SL!M finds it important that children and young people have accessible opportunities to experience culture. Free access to all museums in Leeuwarden for residents under 18 encourages learning, curiosity, and involvement with art and heritage, making culture accessible to all.

230271.explanation: SL!M considers safety important, but businesses are responsible for their own premises. The municipality can advise or support joint initiatives, but providing extra funds to individual companies is unfair and does not fit within municipal resources.

230272.explanation: SL!M believes that sustainability in agriculture should be promoted. Financial support helps farmers invest in environmentally friendly techniques and future-proof production. This way, the municipality contributes to a better environment, sustainable food chain, and livable region.

230274.explanation: SL!M opposes advertising for alcohol and gambling in public spaces. It increases the risk of problems, especially among youth. Organizations like VNN emphasize prevention. Advertising should be limited or banned for a healthier, safer municipality.

230275.explanation: SL!M believes that the income threshold for poverty assistance in Leeuwarden should be raised. This allows more people with financial problems to receive support and participate in society. It is an investment in equality, opportunities, and the well-being of our residents.

230277.explanation: SL!M recognizes that street lighting outside built-up areas can enhance safety, but dark areas protect nature and tranquility. Smart lighting, such as motion sensors, can provide a good balance. Therefore, we do not take a definitive stance for or against.

230278.explanation: SL!M believes every child should start the school day healthy, regardless of income. Free school breakfasts at primary schools reduce hunger, aid concentration and atmosphere, and reduce inequality of opportunity. This is an investment in health and equal chances.

230279.explanation: SL!M believes that waste collection fees should be reduced to ease housing costs. Other municipalities have shown this is possible. More efficient collection and promoting waste separation can lower costs without losing quality.

230281.explanation: SL!M believes that children and youth must have a voice with the municipality. A youth ombudsman provides a low-threshold contact point for complaints and concerns, builds trust, and shows that young people’s opinions count.

230284.explanation: SL!M believes that everyone should be able to participate in sports and culture. Extra allowances for residents with low incomes make sports and cultural visits possible, strengthen social contacts, and ensure equal opportunities in Leeuwarden.

230285.explanation: SL!M believes that Dutch youth and refugees should be able to live together. Shared housing locations offer guidance, structure, and social interaction. Investing in this strengthens safety, solidarity, and personal development in Leeuwarden.

275822.explanation: SL!M believes that elderly people should live comfortably and independently as long as possible. Care is sometimes necessary, but having one's own place is more important. Living arrangements like Knarrenhof therefore fit well with SL!M's vision.

275823.explanation: SL!M believes that sustainability must be accessible to everyone. Energy coaches help residents save energy, reduce housing costs, and make sustainable choices. This prevents sustainability from being available only to those with money.

275824.explanation: Opening the Prins Hendrik Bridge is very expensive. The potential increase in watersport tourists does not justify these costs. Additionally, it is a busy spot, and opening could cause traffic jams.

275825.explanation: There is a huge legal battle surrounding this location. The municipality would be better off focusing on finding another suitable site, so attention can be on construction rather than litigation. The shortage of shelter places calls for decisiveness and action.

275826.explanation: If the national government neglects care, the municipality, according to human rights, is obliged to act and provide shelter.

275827.explanation: SL!M believes Leeuwarden must take responsibility for providing shelter. There is currently a shortage of suitable places. SL!M supports a new asylum seekers center but wants the neighborhood involved from the very start of planning. Not just informing, but genuinely engaging in dialogue (Einstein model).

230227.explanation: A referendum—especially with a simple yes/no question—is vulnerable to populist politics. Therefore, PvdD wants to approach referenda cautiously, favors citizen assemblies more, and does not think the 300-signature threshold should be lowered.

230229.explanation: Taxes are necessary for public services and sustainability. It's easy to score points during election time by calling for tax cuts. Reductions are only desirable if they do not lead to cuts in nature, healthcare, or social support.

230230.explanation: Tourism must not come at the expense of livability, nature, and the environment. Tourist tax helps fairly distribute the social costs of tourism.

230231.explanation: The dog tax is a rather arbitrary tax. Owning a pet should not necessarily be taxed. If the dog tax is maintained, revenues should be used exclusively for dog-related facilities.

230232.explanation: The policy on closing times is the result of years of searching for the best solution. At this moment, we see no reason to reopen this discussion.

230233.explanation: The PvdD wants to establish a new municipal energy company that focuses, among other things, on supporting/financing citizen initiatives.

230234.explanation: There are criteria by which different groups of people can have priority for social rental housing. Status holders are one of those groups.

230235.explanation: Of course! Language is important for participation in society. The municipality should certainly invest in this.

230236.explanation: There are too many examples of exploitation of labor migrants not to want to address it.

230237.explanation: Of course. When it comes to the safety of trees along a road: the danger is not caused by the trees, but by the driving behavior of motorists.

230238.explanation: Wood stoves are an important source of air pollution.

230239.explanation: Fireworks cause animal suffering, injuries, and environmental damage. The emissions from fireworks are enormous. Limiting them contributes to safety and well-being. There are definitely better (cleaner) ways to welcome the New Year, for example with light shows and drones.

230241.explanation: The PvdD wants children to not only receive Frisian language lessons 'because they have to,' but also to learn about Frisian history, sports, and culture. From there, interest in the Frisian language can also develop. Currently, education is still too much focused on the Netherlands and the Randstad.

230243.explanation: At most near schools. Coffeeshops are not banned in the Netherlands, so not in Leeuwarden either. If coffeeshops cause nuisance, action must be taken against it. Banning them out of fear of nuisance is putting the cart before the horse.

230244.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety but affects privacy. The PvdD prefers human work. We prefer enough street coaches, enforcement officers, and police rather than cameras. At most, we allow limited, targeted, and proportional camera surveillance in certain areas, with a clear evaluation.

230246.explanation: In a municipality with an above-average number of people with low incomes, having enough social rental housing is a priority.

230247.explanation: We live in a beautiful province and must be careful with space. So building upwards is fine, but... not too high, like the Avéro Tower. Human scale matters.

230248.explanation: A home is to live in, not to make money from.

230249.explanation: Fine, but not only for students. Other target groups can also benefit from converting vacant office buildings.

230251.explanation: Many people on benefits are willing to do activities like volunteer work as a 'counter-performance.' Others often have too many problems that prevent activities. Help people overcome their problems, and they will naturally become active participants in society again.

230252.explanation: That is often a fine stepping stone to regular work.

230253.explanation: It is a disgrace that there are still people with incomes that are too low. Let's solve that so everyone can contribute to municipal revenues.

230254.explanation: Some neighborhoods are full of cars. There is no room for children to play on the street. The PvdD wants fewer parking spaces and to 'green' the space that is freed up.

230255.explanation: If we improve public transport and bike paths towards the city center, people won't need to come by car anymore.

230256.explanation: We would have preferred that entrepreneurs took responsibility and used clean trucks and vans. Since that is not the case, the municipality will have to impose a ban.

230257.explanation: There must be enough charging points. If that is not the case: yes, more must be installed.

230258.explanation: In a village like Grou, parking pressure is sometimes very high. Paid parking can be a solution there.

230259.explanation: It is ridiculous how cars now drive and park everywhere in the city center. More car-restricted zones. More enforcement too!

230260.explanation: A community center or village hall can meet an important need. Where needed, we support neighborhood and village centers.

230261.explanation: The PvdD is not against the idea of shared space, but it doesn't work at the station, so we choose safety and clarity there.

230262.explanation: We have to deal with noise standards, and the road lies close to buildings. The idea that 70 km/h is better because drivers will then keep the speed is an incorrect argument in our view.

230263.explanation: In times of housing shortage, vacancy is unacceptable, but rather than imposing fines everywhere, we solve the nitrogen crisis and build homes that we keep out of the 'housing market.'

230265.explanation: Stimulating Frisian heritage is fine, but we wonder what that 'extra money' will yield and how much that would be.

230268.explanation: A good plan like BOSK can be implemented with municipal funds. Whether that is more or less than the current budget will be judged based on a plan. Good plans should be realized.

230270.explanation: To invite young people and expose them to art and cultural heritage, free admission is a good idea. However, we will make this dependent on available financial resources.

230271.explanation: The safety of company premises is the responsibility of those companies themselves. The municipality ensures the safety of public spaces.

230272.explanation: Yes! Farmers are let down nationally and provincially. So it is up to the municipality to offer farmers perspective. We want to look with farmers at less livestock farming and more (organic) agriculture.

230274.explanation: Certainly not! Gambling addiction and (excessive) alcohol use cause a lot of social misery. We also no longer tolerate 'fossil' advertising for, for example, air travel.

230275.explanation: The PvdD goes further: in principle, the municipality should not set financial limits at all. Poverty is rarely just one problem. Look at what someone needs. It is seldom only money.

230277.explanation: Leeuwarden causes a lot of light pollution, and the PvdD wants to reduce that. For safety, lighting is chosen that disturbs nature less.

230278.explanation: In principle, this is not a municipal duty, but in some parts of Leeuwarden this is not going well, so we want to provide for it.

230279.explanation: The polluter pays. What we do support is paying based on the waste you produce: the more waste, the higher the costs; the less, the lower.

230281.explanation: This is important to the PvdD! However, an ombuds function must also have powers to tackle issues that need addressing. No paper tiger!

230284.explanation: Priority is of course for basic needs (rent, electricity, and groceries), but sport and culture are also important. By the way, we also want reimbursement for veterinary care for pets for people with low incomes.

230285.explanation: This sounds rather forced. Yes, it is nice if Dutch people and refugees end up living on the same location (instead of segregation), but steering it this way goes too far.

275822.explanation: The PvdD is positive about such living arrangements, also called 'knarrenhof.' However, it is not the role of the municipality to co-finance a home. Elderly people rent or buy themselves and can use the care organized for everyone in the municipality.

275823.explanation: Energy coaches still prove important for people with low incomes. If there are not enough, more should be appointed.

275824.explanation: Making the Prins Hendrik Bridge movable is a nice idea but also costs a lot of money. Money that could be spent on other things too. For the PvdD, making it movable is not a priority.

275825.explanation: The Party for the Animals (PvdD) has no objection to the location at the airbase. However, we do have a problem with expanding the airbase at that location. The base is large enough, and the Ministry of Defense should consider whether they can use all that space more efficiently.

275826.explanation: Leeuwarden has long supported asylum seekers whose applications have been rejected, mainly because these people usually have nowhere to go: return is often not permitted by their country of origin. It is a matter of humanity to prevent inhumane situations from arising.

275827.explanation: Leeuwarden has a long tradition of hosting asylum seekers. The positive experiences with this increasingly call for Leeuwarden to build a solid, permanent asylum seekers' center, partly to replace the Heliconweg location.

230227.explanation: Citizens simply have the right to more say.

230229.explanation: The municipality overcharged too much last year and sent an embarrassing letter to all entrepreneurs stating that the money would not be returned to them but would remain with the municipality. (which the old 'current' council also agreed with).

230231.explanation: It depends on how the money is spent; it must be used for the animals and can partly finance an animal hospital here, structured as a foundation with much lower veterinary costs.

230232.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be given a bit more freedom here; this also ensures more distribution, which makes the city more livable. And we expect this will cause less nuisance.

230233.explanation: Because otherwise it would not be affordable for everyone.

230234.explanation: No, because other citizens have been on waiting lists for years; it is unfair if someone who has lived in the Netherlands for a shorter time gets priority. Due to the housing shortage, it is already common for someone to be on a waiting list for 5 or 10 years.

230235.explanation: The national government should contribute more here.

230236.explanation: Only with policy that offers qualitatively good living spaces.

230237.explanation: A green environment is healthy for its inhabitants, both humans and animals. It also helps to combat heat.

230238.explanation: Because some homes have no other heating systems, and we do not want to cause unnecessary costs for citizens.

230239.explanation: At a central location near the Oldehove. Additionally, we expect that enforcing the new fireworks laws of the national government will be impossible, and designated fireworks zones should be allowed where citizens have the opportunity to enjoy their hobby once a year.

230241.explanation: We are the capital of Friesland, but residents should be able to choose whether they want to use Frisian or Dutch as their language.

230244.explanation: Ensures more safety; perpetrators are caught more quickly.

230246.explanation: There is a shortage of affordable rental housing.

230247.explanation: Use the limited space we have sparingly.

230248.explanation: In times of housing scarcity, Airbnb is undesirable; besides, it unnecessarily drives up rents and house prices.

230252.explanation: We expect this will eventually lead to more permanent contracts and help reduce poverty.

230254.explanation: Differs per neighborhood and also involves residents in expressing their wishes.

230255.explanation: If it helps our entrepreneurs (shops, hospitality), we encourage it.

230256.explanation: Not every entrepreneur can afford to buy a new company car.

230257.explanation: Steer according to supply and demand.

230258.explanation: Involve residents of the villages. For example, in Stiens there are too few parking spaces; a parking garage could be a solution. Use part of the revenues for marketing local entrepreneurs.

230259.explanation: The city center is already struggling due to the rise of the internet.

230260.explanation: Provides connection and helps combat loneliness among citizens, young and old.

230261.explanation: This area was extensively renovated a few years ago; now only some minor adjustments like the crossing are needed.

230262.explanation: Only if it can be done safely, and input from local residents is also desirable.

230263.explanation: Vacant buildings cause decay, insecurity, and loss of livability. This can and must be different. Vacancy is a waste of space in a time of housing shortage.

230268.explanation: We prefer permanent new artworks rather than disposable art, and mixed events through the city entrepreneurs program (3FM Glass House 2028, Christmas parade 2027, national Sinterklaas arrival, and many more).

230270.explanation: Art and culture are important for children, which are not affordable for every family.

230272.explanation: But then together with the national government.

230274.explanation: Most people are wise enough to decide this for themselves.

230275.explanation: Poverty must be tackled in the city; it really needs to decrease.

230277.explanation: Provides more safety. But wisdom must be used to consider the environment, light pollution, and impact on nature.

230278.explanation: There are currently too many children from low-income families going to school without food.

230279.explanation: This leads to more litter and dumping, which causes deterioration, insecurity, pests, mice infestations, rats, and loss of livability. With lower costs, we expect this to decrease.

230281.explanation: Young people also have the right to an opinion and help. It concerns their future.

230284.explanation: Must be accessible to everyone, and sports also contribute to better health. Prevents social exclusion.

230285.explanation: For better integration, but it must be safe. In case of nuisance, The Hague will take them back.

275822.explanation: Everyone has the right to live in their own living environment.

275823.explanation: Guided by supply and demand. Preference to help residents with low income.

275824.explanation: Depends on the costs and benefits. And residents must be able to have a say about this.

275826.explanation: The chance is then greater that people from other municipalities will also come to Leeuwarden. It also increases the chance of more nuisance.

275827.explanation: There are already more than enough.

230227.explanation: MERA25 favors more citizen influence. This must be better facilitated by the municipality. However, for important binding decisions, MERA25 supports an absolute majority of votes (75%).

230229.explanation: The property tax must be reduced based on income and assets. But it should not be reduced for large fortunes.

230230.explanation: This tax is passed on to the consumer, while tourists are actually very valuable for our local economy.

230231.explanation: The municipality does not clean up dog poop anyway, so what are you paying for? There are also no poop bags at the dog walking areas. If it must remain, make the dog tax income-dependent.

230232.explanation: MERA25 believes that there should be some liveliness in Leeuwarden. Especially opportunities must be offered to young people. Therefore, MERA25 supports extended opening hours for hospitality and culture.

230233.explanation: Energy cooperatives are good, but sustainability must be affordable for everyone. Subsidies should be targeted at people who really need them. Social rental homes must also be made more sustainable.

230234.explanation: In the past 50 years, too few homes have been built due to neoliberal policies, and we have learned that keeping refugees outside society is inhumane and does not promote good integration. Everyone must be able to participate.

230235.explanation: Language lessons must be accessible to everyone!

230236.explanation: There is a great risk that good living conditions are not guaranteed or lack sufficient supervision. The same applies to supervision of working hours regulations. Therefore, it should not be mandatory to provide housing on one's own business premises.

230237.explanation: MERA25 strongly supports more greenery in neighborhoods and along roads because we believe that more visible nature also benefits people's mental health. We believe residents cannot feel truly at home among concrete buildings and bare streets. More greenery!

230238.explanation: Until a good transition is possible, this must remain allowed. The use of fireplaces and wood stoves should not be banned until there are other good heating solutions for everyone.

230239.explanation: Fireworks are bad for the environment, expensive, and scare our pets. MERA25 opposes the consumer-oriented way of selling fireworks. Therefore, it is better that the municipality organizes the fireworks show.

230241.explanation: MERA25 sees the local and social context in which people live as one of the most important values. As a grassroots movement, MERA25 protects local traditions, language, and culture. Therefore, MERA25 wholeheartedly supports more attention to Frisian language and culture.

230243.explanation: Pickup counters must be established outside the center to prevent cars from driving into and out of the city center at coffeeshops. The older coffeeshops in the center should precisely be preserved as social centers and meeting places! They fulfill a valuable social function.

230244.explanation: MERA25 is against the surveillance state. Over the past 10 years, we have seen a 30 percent decrease in crime, and the living environment does not feel safer with more cameras. Everyone has the right to privacy.

230246.explanation: As much social rental housing as possible must be added. Housing is a right, and MERA25 finds it disgraceful that this has been left to the market. 30% is too little. MERA25 strongly supports collective housing options, extra social rental housing, and tolerance policies for alternative living.

230247.explanation: Residential high-rises are a sustainable, efficient solution for the housing shortage. We want more social rental apartments and support vertical expansion of living space over horizontal. We also believe that every apartment building should have common areas available. More flats!

230248.explanation: Housing is for people, not for profit. Slumlords must be fought against.

230249.explanation: It is a disgrace that large office buildings stand empty for years during the current housing crisis.

230251.explanation: Everyone must do socially meaningful work, not only people with social assistance benefits. Away with meaningless jobs. The business sector is full of socially useless work. Much volunteer work is socially very meaningful and also offers social contact and development.

230252.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. However, we believe jobs must be socially and socially useful. It is unnecessary to sponsor the commercial business sector even more.

230253.explanation: The exemption must be expanded. It is needed more than ever. Many families live at or below the poverty line, and their numbers are only growing. We also believe that municipal taxes should be income-dependent.

230254.explanation: Parking can also be built vertically! Parking permits are very expensive now, which calls for redesigning parking spaces and better solutions.

230255.explanation: This is not Amsterdam. Parking must remain affordable for everyone.

230256.explanation: MERA25 supports greening, but the center of Leeuwarden is already low-traffic and the highways nearby are so close that banning commercial and freight vehicles from the center hardly affects residents' health. It is also very impractical for businesses.

230257.explanation: We support vertical parking garages (above or underground) with enough electric charging stations.

230258.explanation: Parking fees should only be charged to encourage public transport, not merely to fill the state treasury.

230259.explanation: MERA25 stands for a green future, aiming to return to a time when the city center was not filled with flashy billboards and unsafe roads where cars speed through. The city of the future is accessible to everyone, where MERA25 wants to focus on good and affordable public transport.

230260.explanation: Neighborhood centers are the bonding factor in neighborhoods and villages. They are a crucial key in preventing loneliness and poverty, as well as important for culture and social atmosphere. Every neighborhood and village should have one or more well-accessible, non-commercial community centers and/or youth centers.

230261.explanation: In our view, the shared space works perfectly fine. Tearing everything up and rebuilding it again costs enormous amounts of money that could be invested elsewhere. The previous council should have considered this more carefully.

230262.explanation: It is a dual-lane access road to the highway, there are no adjacent homes, and people already systematically drive too fast there, which actually creates dangerous situations when someone does comply with the 50 km/h limit.

230263.explanation: A fine is not sufficient; in addition, MERA25 believes that vacant properties should be used for social initiatives such as giving-away shops, socio-cultural centers, anti-squatting, alternative housing forms, or asylum seeker shelters.

230265.explanation: If more money goes to associations that practice Frisian sports, we hope these sports will become more accessible to a larger audience.

230268.explanation: The funds for Arcadia should be better distributed among multiple cultural initiatives.

230270.explanation: The first thing lost for families with lower incomes is social contacts and outings for the children. Therefore, it is very important that there are free activities accessible to everyone.

230271.explanation: Companies are required to take measures themselves to ensure and improve safety on their premises.

230272.explanation: It is necessary for farmers to become more sustainable, and we must continue to invest in research into new solutions. However, strict conditions must be imposed so that these subsidies are actually used for sustainability.

230274.explanation: Advertisements should be abolished, and we also find it disgusting that the government exploits gambling addiction to fill the state treasury.

230275.explanation: If people are already struggling to make ends meet and need help, this must be provided. This allows the municipality to act preventively before someone ends up in debt restructuring.

230277.explanation: The bike paths must be well lit, but we must also consider the ecosystems and animals living there, so solutions like green lights and other nature-friendly options are necessary.

230278.explanation: All children must be able to participate and everyone deserves equal opportunities. Groceries are becoming unaffordable for low incomes. Free breakfast at school for children from families with lower incomes is a very good idea.

230279.explanation: Waste collection fees should be income-dependent, so higher incomes pay proportionally more than lower incomes. Additionally, pay-per-collection encourages littering.

230281.explanation: Children and youth must be heard! MERA25 supports an extensive neighborhood network of street coaches and neighborhood teams involving the youth ombudsman. This way, youth can easily file complaints about the municipality and signals are picked up faster.

230284.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Not only should more money go to the Adult Fund for sports and culture, applying for this scheme must also be made more accessible! Additionally, many more free activities must be organized in neighborhoods.

230285.explanation: MERA25 believes in a multicultural society and sees that it is beneficial for people to come into contact with other cultures at a young age. This contributes to solidarity.

275822.explanation: Knarrenhofjes (senior cohousing courtyards) are the future.

275823.explanation: Energy coaches should focus on efficient energy use in cooperation with housing corporations. This ensures the help is truly applicable to the individual living situation.

275824.explanation: We want to see if there is broader support for this in the municipality through a survey. If a majority of residents value this adjustment, the municipality must implement it!

275825.explanation: The noise nuisance next to the airbase does not contribute to humane living conditions.

275826.explanation: Even rejected asylum seekers have human rights. They are therefore entitled to good shelter and meaningful activities. MERA25 supports good and dignified shelter.

275827.explanation: Every municipality must contribute proportionally to shelter. Better facilities are also needed. Often, the current living conditions in shelters violate human rights.

statements.title: The municipality should encourage more attention to Frisian at all primary schools.
statements.moreInfo: The core goals for education are set nationally. Schools have room to determine how to meet them. The municipality cannot force extra attention for Frisian, but it can encourage and support it, for example by making funds available.

statements.title: Paid parking in the municipality should become cheaper.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
statements.moreInfo: From the coming New Year’s, a national ban on setting off most types of consumer fireworks will apply in the Netherlands. These rules are made by the national government and apply to all municipalities. The ban does not apply to professional fireworks. Municipalities may decide to organize a fireworks display on New Year’s Eve; the municipality arranges and pays for such a display itself.

statements.theme: Priority for people with refugee status
statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country because of war or personal persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. If they receive a residence permit, they must move from an asylum seekers’ centre to social housing. Municipalities can choose to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing. The municipality of Leeuwarden is currently doing this.

statements.title: It should be easier for residents to request a referendum, for example by requiring fewer signatures; currently 300 signatures are needed.

titleParts.text: Het moet makkelijker worden voor inwoners een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

titleParts.text: aan te vragen, bijvoorbeeld met minder handtekeningen. Nu zijn daar 300 handtekeningen voor nodig.

statements.title: The municipality should plant more trees, including along main roads.
In the municipality of Leeuwarden the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed, even in bad weather.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Leeuwarden moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

titleParts.text: toegestaan blijven, ook bij slecht weer.

statements.moreInfo: Open fireplaces and wood stoves emit smoke and fine particulate matter. Municipalities can set rules on their use, for example under certain weather conditions.

statements.title: At least 40% of newly built homes in the municipality of Leeuwarden should be social rental housing; the municipality currently aims for 30%.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente Leeuwarden moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. Nu streeft de gemeente naar 30%.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants in Leeuwarden’s city center should be able to set their own closing times.
statements.moreInfo: What time a café or restaurant must close currently depends on the location in Leeuwarden. Different rules apply for different locations.

statements.title: There should be more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.moreInfo: Subsidized jobs, also called basic jobs, are positions where the municipality pays part of the wage costs for people who have been out of work for a long time. Leeuwarden currently has 25 basic jobs available.

statements.title: More car-free streets should be created in Leeuwarden’s city center.
All villages and neighborhoods within the municipality should have their own social and cultural community center.
The municipality should modify the Prins Hendrik Bridge between the center and the station so that it can open.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if the roof is suitable, or apply for a permit for a wind turbine in the garden if it is large enough. If that is not possible, people can jointly buy or rent land for one or more wind turbines or solar panels so they can still invest in sustainable energy. Sustainable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind. Municipalities can offer various forms of support for this, such as subsidies or loans. At the moment the municipality of Leeuwarden is running a number of experiments supporting energy cooperatives.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.moreInfo: Leeuwarden currently has twelve coffeeshops. The sale of soft drugs such as marijuana in coffeeshops is tolerated; that is national policy. Toleration (gedogen) means something is illegal under the law but is nevertheless allowed by the police. Municipalities may decide how many coffeeshops they allow and where they may be located.

statements.title: Commercial and freight trucks that emit pollutants should be banned from the center of Leeuwarden.
statements.moreInfo: A zero-emission zone is an area in a city where cars that emit harmful substances are not allowed.

statements.title: The municipality of Leeuwarden should provide shelter to asylum seekers who are not allowed to remain in the Netherlands.
