statements.moreInfo: People who flee their country because of war or personal persecution can apply for asylum in the Netherlands. The Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) assesses their application and decides whether they may stay in the Netherlands. During that procedure these asylum seekers must be accommodated. If the asylum request is rejected, former asylum seekers must leave the Netherlands. That usually cannot be done immediately, but they no longer have a right to accommodation. Some municipalities offer simple shelter for this group, commonly called "bed-bath-bread" shelter, which provides only a place to sleep, food and a shower.

statements.title: The municipality should appoint a youth ombudsman where children and young people can bring complaints about the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: An independent person who helps children and young people with complaints about the government.

titleParts.text: aanstellen waar kinderen en jongeren terecht kunnen met klachten over de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should again create separate areas for pedestrians, cyclists and motorists at the station.
statements.moreInfo: The area around the station in Leeuwarden was arranged a few years ago as a so-called shared space. That means pedestrians, cyclists and cars use the same space without clear separation.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Leeuwarden.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a fine for people who leave their homes vacant for a long time.
The waste collection charge should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Municipal tax for collecting and processing household waste.

statements.moreInfo: The waste collection levy is the municipal tax for collecting and processing household waste. Municipalities determine the amount themselves. If the waste collection levy is reduced, the municipality must compensate by cutting spending or increasing income in another way.

statements.title: The municipality should spend less money on the cultural event Arcadia.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. In some municipalities they are required to perform a counter-performance. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid.

statements.title: Residents with low incomes should receive more funding for sports and culture.
statements.moreInfo: Leeuwarden is currently affiliated with the Adult Fund for Sport & Culture. Residents with a low income can receive a reimbursement from this fund for, for example, a subscription or equipment. The maximum contribution from this fund is €300 per person per year.

statements.title: The speed limit on the Overijsselselaan should be raised from 50 to 70 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

titleParts.text: op de Overijsselselaan moet omhoog van 50 naar 70 km/u.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more in energy coaches who help residents make their homes more sustainable.
Advertisements for gambling and alcohol in public spaces should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: Reclames voor gokken en alcohol in de

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.

statements.moreInfo: Advertising in public space is seen on, for example, billboards and digital screens. Municipalities can set their own rules, in addition to national rules, about where and in what form advertising in public space is allowed.

statements.title: The municipality should provide extra funding to clubs that practice Frisian sports.
A new asylum seekers’ center should be established in our municipality.

shootoutStatements.title: More tall apartment buildings should be allowed in the municipality of Leeuwarden.
There should be more parking spaces in residential neighborhoods.
The tourist tax should be lowered.
The municipality should invest in free language lessons for refugees with a residence permit.
The municipality should provide more street lighting outside the city and villages.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
The municipality should provide free breakfasts at primary schools.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.
Vacant offices should be converted into student housing using municipal funds.
The municipality should install more charging stations for electric cars.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

shootoutStatements.theme: Free museum entry up to age 18
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should fund free museum access for residents under 18.
Parking in the villages should remain free.
The municipality should co-fund housing options for the elderly that allow them to live at home and have access to care.
The municipality should give money to farmers who want to make their operations more sustainable.
Companies that jointly want to improve safety on their sites should receive more funding from the municipality.
The municipality should help more people by raising the income threshold for qualifying for poverty assistance.
The municipality should look for a location for an asylum seekers' centre (AZC) other than next to the airbase.
The municipality should invest more in locations where Dutch young people can live with refugees who have a residence permit.
